author: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice
book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice.
The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.

metadata.author: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice

title: Canon XVIII.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: That women ought not to live in bishops’ houses, nor in monasteries of men.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: “Be ye without offence to those who are without,” says the divine Apostle.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Now for women to live in Bishops’ houses or in monasteries is ground for grave offence.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Whoever therefore is known to have a female slave or freewoman in the episcopal palace or in a monastery for the discharge of some service, let him be rebuked.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: And if he still continue to retain her, let him be deposed.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: If it happens that women are on the suburban estates, and the bishop or hegumenos desires to go thither, so long as the bishop or hegumenos is present, let no woman at that time continue her work, but let her betake herself to some other place until the bishop [or hegumenos] has departed, so that there be no occasion of complaint.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: It is not fitting that women should be kept in episcopal houses or in monasteries.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: If anyone shall dare to do so, he shall be reproved; but if he persists, he shall be deposed.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: No woman is allowed to serve or even to appear where a bishop or a superior of a monastery is present, but let her keep herself apart until he be gone.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Every woman the present canon expels from the Episcopium or bishop’s house, agreeably to Novel CXXIII, chapter 29, of the Emperor Justinian, which, (although the Nicene canon on the subject makes a mother, sister, daughter and other persons free from all suspicions, exceptions), admits no exceptions in the case of a bishop, but says, “We allow no bishop to have any woman or to live with one.”

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: For as bishops are set in a higher grade above the rest of the clergy, and ought to be like lights set on a candlestick to give light, rightly they are ordered more than others to take care to avoid all appearance of evil, and to remove all from them that might cause suspicion.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: With regard to monks and their houses see Justinian’s Novel CXXXIII., Cap.
